title: new quadlet docs are wrong and are missing many imporant sections
body:
Now that I tried to look up some quadlet info that I knew was in the docs I noticed that the rewrite removed almost all of the important details of how quadlet actually work.

compare the new page https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html

with the old one https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.6.1/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html

Many sections are just gone, no docs on how to debug, no docs about the service type, the section about enabling the files is missing (instead the new site claims `systemctl enable` works), relative paths, symbolic notices and much more were simply removed and are nowhere to be found.

The new podman-quadlet-basic-usage is also inconsistent and recommend systemctl enable which cannot work.

comments.body:
cc @jankaluza @ygalblum @baude @mheon 


I must also add that I am completely dissatisfied with the review process of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26929, IMO the state of the docs are much worse now and they recommend outright incorrect things and the AI  hallucinations clearly didn't help in terms of quality.

The deduplication is certainly nice but the way the quadlet docs are worded now is a major downgrade IMO and must be fixed.

@Luap99 You are correct. Sorry. I mostly looked at the structure and deduplication assuming that the information itself did not change.
I think the right this to do at this point is to revert the changes and have them fixes and reviewed again from scratch
I would be in favour of reverting, though I guess we should give @jankaluza (or someone else) some time to fix it properly first? Not sure if reverting + fixing it again is any better.
I suggested reverting because fixing and doing a proper review can take time. During that time, we don't want to have faulty documentation

> I suggested reverting because fixing and doing a proper review can take time. During that time, we don't want to have faulty documentation

I agree I make a PR to revert it for now then
I'll prepare new podman-systemd.unit.5 tomorrow. +1 for revert.
@Luap99 , I'm sorry for that. I think I focused too much on the deduplication in the end and completely missed that part of original man-page... :-(
